messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 is open
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

We are pleased to announce an upcoming livestream seminar designed to enhance your data analysis capabilities. Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 16, led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This seminar provides researchers with the practical skills to implement advanced clustering algorithms and dimensionality reduction techniques using R and Python. By mastering methods like PCA, t-SNE, and UMAP, you can effectively resolve the curse of dimensionality and extract meaningful patterns from complex genetic datasets. These powerful unsupervised learning workflows will enable you to translate high-dimensional biological data into robust, publication-ready scientific insights.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/clustering-high-dimensional-data-30) to join the livestream, and please pass this along to colleagues and students who may find it useful!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling in R
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - Enhance your quantitative toolkit for analyzing complex biological and genetic relationships. Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 11 with Holmes Finch (Department of Educational Psychology - Ball State University). This seminar provides the theoretical and practical foundations to specify and estimate complex, non-linear relationships among latent and observed variables using R and Mplus. Geneticists can apply these advanced modeling techniques to map intricate pathways, gene-environment interactions, and non-linear growth trajectories in phenotypic data. By integrating these robust structural equation models into your R workflows, you can better capture the biological realities of complex traits and accelerate your genomic research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/nonlinear-structural-equation-modeling) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 starts soon
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 starts soon
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - Enhance your analytical toolkit with advanced methods for managing complex, multi-dimensional datasets. Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 16 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This upcoming livestream seminar provides comprehensive training in advanced clustering techniques and dimensionality reduction using R and Python. Genetics researchers can apply these methods to extract meaningful patterns from complex, high-dimensional genomic datasets. By mastering algorithms like DBSCAN and UMAP, you can significantly enhance your statistical workflows and accelerate discoveries in computational biology.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/clustering-high-dimensional-data-30) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Developing R/Bioconductor packages
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce the online course ?Developing R/Bioconductor packages for genomics?, taking place 6?10 July (2?6 pm Berlin time).
 
Course website:[ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-packages/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-packages/ ) 
 
 
This 5-day hands-on course is designed to make R package development and the Bioconductor ecosystem accessible to biologists and early-stage bioinformaticians who want to move from package users to package developers. The focus is on building robust, reproducible, and distributable genomic software in R.
 
Participants will learn how to:
Develop well-structured R functions and packages
Document, test, and maintain code effectively
Manage dependencies, versions, and continuous integration (GitHub Actions)
Build Bioconductor-compliant packages and objects
Create vignettes and package websites
Submit, release, and disseminate packages (including JOSS and rOpenSci pathways)
The course combines short lectures (~2h/day), live demonstrations, and extensive guided exercises with solutions. Daily office hours and a dedicated Slack channel will support interaction and Q&A.
 
 
Best regards,
 
The Physalia-courses website
 
[ www.physalia-courses.org ]( http://www.physalia-courses.org )
 
 
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Analyzing Latent Variable Mixture Models
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Enhance your statistical toolkit with advanced methods for modeling unobserved population heterogeneity. Latent Variable Mixture Models is a 2-day seminar livestreaming July 6?7, led by Holmes Finch (Department of Educational Psychology - Ball State University). Researchers analyzing complex genetic datasets can apply these advanced modeling techniques to identify unobserved subpopulations and model nonnormal trait distributions within their study cohorts. By integrating these methods into your R workflows, you can better resolve population heterogeneity and detect measurement noninvariance across diverse genetic samples. This approach allows for more robust inference when linking genetic markers to complex, multi-layered phenotypes.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/latent-variable-mixture-models) to register, and we would appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
